dominant_event: MCX single-trade −₹31,246 (3.1% of capital) — stale-price/unverified-CMP LIMIT BUY at ₹8,400 vs live CMP ₹3,193, on a same-session-earnings name (4 PM call), with reasoning that ended 'deferring this — without confirmed CMP d…' but action emitted BUY.

changes.summary: CMP/LTP VERIFICATION — DO NOT TRADE ON ESTIMATED PRICES
details: Mandates entry/exit prices grounded in current-cycle LTP/CMP fields, not memory or estimates.
5% sanity-check on |submitted_price − data_LTP|/data_LTP; gap > 5% routes to NO_ACTION.
NO_ACTION when LTP/CMP field is missing for the symbol in this cycle.
Watchlist_research blob noted as valid fallback CMP source (it routinely quotes live price in recent_news prose).
Prohibited-phrases list ('estimated entry pending verification', 'without confirmed CMP', etc.) auto-routes to NO_ACTION.
Failure-mode prior cites today's MCX disaster with specific numbers (entry ₹8,400, CMP ₹3,193, 163% gap, −₹31,246 realized in 33 minutes).
changes.why: MCX BUY at 12:41:15 submitted LIMIT 8400 against live CMP 3193. Bot's own reasoning admitted 'estimated entry pending verification' and ended mid-sentence with 'NOTE: deferring this — without confirmed CMP d…'. The watchlist_research blob for MCX in the same cycle quoted the correct price: 'trading around Rs 3,193 (+3.12%)'. Pre-submit gap-check is the simplest intervention to block this class of stale-price-after-corporate-action entries.
changes.expected_impact: Catastrophic stale-price entries (>5% gap between submitted price and data CMP) become self-blocked at the strategy layer. Single-trade-blowup tail risk from this specific failure mode is closed. Mild cost: some legitimate same-day gappers with >5% intraday range may be filtered into NO_ACTION — acceptable trade for closing a 3%-of-capital tail.

changes.summary: ACTION ↔ REASONING CONSISTENCY — RESPECT YOUR OWN CONCLUSION
details: If reasoning concludes deferring/skipping/aborting/passing, action MUST be NO_ACTION.
Final-step self-check: re-read the LAST SENTENCE of reasoning; if it contains any defer/skip/abort synonym, switch action to NO_ACTION.
When reasoning walks from bullish-preamble to bearish-conclusion mid-paragraph, the CONCLUSION governs.
changes.why: MCX BUY 12:41 reasoning literally ended (truncated mid-word): 'NOTE: deferring this — without confirmed CMP d…' yet emitted action = BUY. The guardrail engine reads numeric fields, not prose, so the prose-level 'NO' did not affect the order. This is a generalizable failure pattern — any reasoning that thinks-out-loud and then reverses course risks the same bug if the schema-fill step pre-populated action=BUY.
changes.expected_impact: Reduces self-contradictory orders landing in the book. Particularly applies to entries where due-diligence pass surfaces a disqualifier mid-paragraph but the action field is already set.

details: Extends Rule 11's 'within 3 trading sessions' inverse-rule with an explicit same-session subcase.
Same-session earnings prints, conference calls today, board-meeting results-by-close, and same-day regulator decisions default to NO_ACTION on new entries (not a sized event-trade).
Requires pre-entry scan of `catalysts` / `recent_news` for same-day items.
Frames MCX as a compound Rule 21 + Rule 23 failure — either alone would have blocked it.
changes.why: MCX research blob in the same cycle as the BUY explicitly listed 'Earnings conference call scheduled today (May 11) at 4 PM IST — management commentary on FY27 trajectory' as the headline catalyst. Entry at 12:41 was ~3 hours pre-call. The entry reasoning did not mention the call; the eventual exit reasoning at 13:14 named it 'earnings call TODAY 4PM is binary event'. Information was equally available at both timestamps.
changes.expected_impact: Closes same-session-binary entry tail risk. Combined with Rule 21, the MCX-class failure has two independent blockers in the prompt.

no_change_decisions.decision: NO durable numeric change this review.
no_change_decisions.reason: The proximate cause was execution-quality (stale CMP / action-reasoning divergence / missed same-session binary), not a numeric parameter. Tightening max_position_pct or max_sl_pct globally would punish all entries for a failure mode that is now closed in prose. Reserve config-yaml mutation for cases where the issue is calibration of a numeric floor/ceiling, not strategic discipline.

no_change_decisions.decision: NO additional overrides from strategy layer. Risk monitor already imposed daily_loss_limit_pct 0.04 and min_confidence 0.60 at 13:30 post-MCX, with explicit plan to retain through 2026-05-12 (CPI + DRREDDY/CIPLA earnings session) and revert-candidate at 2026-05-12 EOD.
no_change_decisions.reason: The risk monitor's response is well-calibrated to the event. Adding strategy-layer overrides would duplicate the risk layer's work and overload the override surface. The strategic root cause is being addressed in the system_prompt layer; the immediate transient is being addressed by the existing risk_config overrides. No double-counting.

open_followups_for_future_reviews: Diagnose the paper-trading fill simulator: LIMIT BUY at ₹8,400 against ₹3,201 ask should fill at ₹3,201, not ₹8,400. Today's behaviour is non-realistic vs a live broker. Suggested fix: in the paper fill layer, cap BUY LIMIT fill price at min(limit, ask × 1.005) — the bid-improvement cap. This would have made the MCX fill ≈ ₹3,201 → P&L approximately flat instead of −₹31,246.

Cross-session SL geometry compression: 4 out of 4 winning-side closes this morning cited 'SL flush with CMP' as the partial rationale. Yesterday's trailing was too aggressive against overnight gap risk. Consider open-of-session SL audit (auto-widen any SL within 1% of CMP back to 1.5%+, or auto-exit cleanly).

EXIT-then-MODIFY stale-state pattern: 5+ BLOCKED MODIFYs today on already-exited positions (PNBHOUSING, CAMS, WOCKPHARMA, HFCL, MCX). Rule 12 in prompt, plus probable position-state cache invalidation issue in the engine. Strategy-side cannot fully fix this without engine cooperation.
Min_confidence 0.60 override timeline: zero 0.50-0.59 band admit attempts in 120 min of override life. Schedule hard revert review for 2026-05-13 if 2026-05-12 closes cleanly.
